title: 7 Free AI Tools Every Indian Student Should Use in 2026
summary: From writing assignments to exam prep, these 7 free AI tools can save Indian students hours every week — no subscription required.
content:
# 7 Free AI Tools Every Indian Student Should Use in 2026

Whether you're in Class 12, engineering college, or MBA — AI tools can save you 3–4 hours every week. Here are 7 tools that are free, no sign-up needed, and actually useful for Indian students.

## 1. Forjit AI — Generate Documents, Plans, and Apps

**Best for:** Assignment writing, project reports, study plans

[Forjit AI](https://www.forjitai.in) is a free Indian AI platform that lets you generate resumes, cover letters, meal plans, and even web apps from a simple text prompt — no account required, no API key needed.

**Student use cases:**
- Generate a structured project report outline in seconds
- Create a personalised study timetable (10 exams, 3 weeks, 6 subjects? Describe it, get a plan)
- Draft internship application emails and cover letters
- Generate quiz questions from your notes to test yourself

**How to use:** Visit [forjitai.in](https://www.forjitai.in) → enter your prompt → get results.

## 2. CGPA Calculator — Know Your Actual GPA Instantly

Different universities use different grading scales — 10-point, 7-point, letter grades. Our [CGPA calculator](/tools/cgpa-calculator.html) converts between all formats and calculates your GPA precisely.

**Why it matters:** Job applications and study abroad applications often ask for GPA on a 4.0 scale. Know your number before the interview.

## 3. Perplexity AI — Research With Sources

**Best for:** Research papers, quick fact-checking

Unlike ChatGPT, Perplexity AI shows you sources with every answer. This makes it usable for academic research — you can verify claims and cite properly.

**Tip for Indian students:** Use it for Indian history, science concepts, and current events. Avoid using it to generate essay text directly — your professors will notice the pattern.

## 4. Forjit Word Counter + Case Converter

For students writing in English:
- [Word Counter](/tools/word-counter.html): Check word count, reading time, character count
- [Case Converter](/tools/case-converter.html): Fix capitalisation errors instantly
- [Lorem Ipsum Generator](/tools/lorem-ipsum.html): Fill placeholder text for design projects

## 5. Canva AI (Free Tier) — Presentations and Posters

**Best for:** Project presentations, science fair posters, college fest banners

Canva's free tier includes AI image generation, background removal, and hundreds of templates. The student plan is free with a college email ID.

## 6. Otter.ai — Record and Transcribe Lectures

**Best for:** Students who miss points while handwriting notes

Otter.ai transcribes lectures in real time. The free plan gives 300 minutes per month — enough for about 5 hours of lectures.

**India note:** Works best in clear English. Accuracy drops with heavy accents or mixed Hindi-English (Hinglish) teaching.

## 7. Age + Date Calculator — For Exam Eligibility

Government exam eligibility often has age cut-offs (UPSC: 21–32, SSC: 18–27). Use our [age calculator](/tools/age-calculator.html) to check exactly where you stand — down to the day.

---

## How to Actually Benefit (Not Just Install and Forget)

The students who benefit most from AI tools do three things:

1. **Use AI for the first draft, not the final draft.** AI-generated content is a starting point. Edit it, add your own examples, your voice.
2. **Fact-check everything.** AI tools can confidently state wrong information. For any statistic or claim you'll submit formally — verify it.
3. **Use AI to learn faster, not to skip learning.** Ask AI to explain a concept 3 different ways. Ask it to quiz you. That's a study superpower.

---
